highlights: OpenAI is advancing its flagship models with GPT-5.6 Sol, alongside strategic partnerships like HP, signaling deep enterprise integration and advanced capabilities in coding and science.

The AI research landscape is witnessing novel architectural developments, including the efficient Wiola SLM and practical diffusion language models for interactive medical drafting, expanding model versatility.

Innovation in AI agents is accelerating, with new multi-agent systems for code summarization, online world modeling, and critical advancements in user-involved permission management and specialized hardware for efficient inference.

agents.title: Agent4cs: A Multi-agent System for Code Summarization in Large Hierarchical Codebases
agents.summary: Introduces a multi-agent framework for summarizing large, complex codebases bottom-up, improving semantic consistency and keyword coverage by leveraging specialized agents for summarization, keyword extraction, and quality assurance.

agents.title: OPINE-World: Programmatic World Modeling with Ontology-error-Prioritized Interactive Exploration
agents.summary: Presents OPINE-World, an LLM agent that learns object-centric programmatic world models online from interaction, demonstrating high skill-acquisition efficiency on tasks like ARC-AGI-3 by coupling hypothesis and test agents.

agents.title: Janus: a Playground for User-Involved Agentic Permission Management
agents.summary: Introduces Janus, a playground system and evaluation framework for user-involved agentic permission management, demonstrating that user input is critical for privacy and security and highlighting the need to account for user behavior like permission fatigue.

models.title: Previewing GPT-5.6 Sol: a next-generation model
models.summary: OpenAI previews GPT-5.6 Sol, a next-generation model featuring stronger capabilities in coding, science, and cybersecurity, coupled with its most advanced safety stack for enhanced performance and security.

models.title: The Wiola Architecture for Efficient Small Language Models
models.summary: Presents Wiola, a fully original Small Language Model (SLM) architecture built from first principles with five novel components, offering a new family of efficient models compatible with HuggingFace Transformers.

models.title: Discrete Diffusion Language Models for Interactive Radiology Report Drafting
models.summary: Adapts a mixture-of-experts diffusion language model, DiffusionGemma-26B, for interactive radiology report drafting, demonstrating competitive performance with faster decoding and a unique any-order infill capability for real-world medical applications.

tools.title: OpenAI and Broadcom unveil LLM-optimized inference chip
tools.summary: OpenAI and Broadcom unveil Jalapeño, a custom AI chip specifically designed for LLM inference, aiming to significantly improve performance, efficiency, and scalability for AI systems.

tools.title: Daybreak: Tools for securing every organization in the world
tools.summary: OpenAI introduces new Daybreak tools, including Codex Security and GPT-5.5-Cyber, designed to help organizations find, validate, and patch vulnerabilities at scale, enhancing cybersecurity posture.

tools.title: Run a vLLM Server on HF Jobs in One Command
tools.summary: Provides a streamlined method to deploy a vLLM server on Hugging Face Jobs with a single command, enabling developers to quickly set up and utilize efficient LLM inference endpoints.

companies.title: HP Inc. launches Frontier strategic partnership with OpenAI
companies.summary: HP Inc. scales its OpenAI Frontier partnership to strategically deploy AI across customer experiences, software development, and enterprise operations, accelerating AI integration in business.

companies.title: Hugging Face and Cerebras bring Gemma 4 to real-time voice AI
companies.summary: Hugging Face and Cerebras collaborate to integrate Gemma 4 into real-time voice AI applications, advancing the deployment of high-performance models for conversational technologies.

companies.title: We’re strengthening our presence in Alabama through new investments and community support.
companies.summary: Google announces a $1.5 billion investment for 2026 and 2027 to expand its data center campus in Jackson County, Alabama, reinforcing its global network infrastructure for AI and cloud services.

learning.title: Ask an AI expert: What exactly is the full stack?
learning.summary: An expert explainer from Google clarifying the 'full-stack' approach to AI, providing foundational knowledge for understanding comprehensive AI system development and deployment.

learning.title: How we used Gemini to build Google I/O 2026
learning.summary: Explores how Google engineers leveraged Gemini AI to produce Google I/O 2026, offering insights and a practical case study on applying advanced AI for complex event production.

learning.title: Beyond LoRA: Can you beat the most popular fine-tuning technique?
learning.summary: An in-depth exploration and comparison of various fine-tuning techniques beyond LoRA, providing practitioners with valuable insights to optimize model performance and efficiency.
